info.title: kameleo-local-api
info.description: You can use the following API endpoints to communicate with the local running Kameleo programmatically.

get.summary:
Retrieves the list of cookies stored in the profile's browser. Note that this list does not include session cookies, which are not
persisted between browser launches.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

post.summary:
Adds a list of cookies to the profile, allowing you to inject cookies before starting the profile. If cookies already exist for a domain,
they will be replaced with the new ones.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

delete.summary: Deletes all the cookies stored in the profile, which will likely log the profile out of all websites.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

get.summary:
Retrieves a set of 25 fingerprints based on specified criteria, with the most recent browser versions at the beginning of the list. Note
that calling this method multiple times will return different sets of fingerprints.

parameters.name: deviceType
parameters.description:
Filter option for the Device Type. Possible values are 'desktop', 'mobile'.
You can also use a comma-separated list to provide more than one value.

parameters.name: osFamily
parameters.description:
Filter option for os family. Possible values are 'windows', 'macos', 'linux', 'android', 'ios'.
You can also use a comma-separated list to provide more than one value.

parameters.name: browserProduct
parameters.description:
Filter option for browser product. Possible values are 'chrome', 'firefox', 'edge', 'safari'.
You can also use a comma-separated list to provide more than one value.

parameters.name: browserVersion
parameters.description:
Filter option for the browser version.
Includes an optional sign (<, >=, etc.) and a major version number (e.g. 135)

get.summary:
Retrieve the complete list of folders in your workspace. Use this endpoint to see the entire folder hierarchy - including subfolders and
unassigned profiles.

Fetch detailed information for a specific folder by its unique GUID. Use this to review the folder’s metadata, contained profiles, and
sharing settings.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder.

put.summary: Updates the details of an existing folder, not including its content.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder.

delete.summary:
Deletes a folder along with all its subfolders. Profiles within the folder will either be deleted or moved to the top-level based on the
query parameters.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder.

parameters.name: includeProfiles
parameters.description: Flag to indicate if the contained profiles should be deleted (true) or moved to the top-level (false).

post.summary:
Create a new folder in your workspace by providing a human-readable name in the request body. Use this to organize your profiles
effectively.
Adds the given profile to the specified folder.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder.

delete.summary: Removes the given profile from the specified folder.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile.

get.summary:
Retrieve the sharing options available for folders. Use this endpoint to review the list of users and roles that can access your folder
resources.

post.summary:
Update the sharing settings for a folder by submitting a list of share access rules (user and role details) in the request body. Use this
to adjust access permissions as needed.

get.summary: Health check request to test if Kameleo has been started and accepting connections.
Fetch detailed information about your user account.

post.summary:
Terminate your current Kameleo session by deauthenticating and exiting the CLI. Use this to safely close your session and release any
associated resources.

get.summary: Gets the list of all the available kernels.

post.summary: Installs a kernel to the local file system.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the kernel.

post.summary: Removes a kernel from the local file system.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the kernel.

409.description: The kernel can't be removed because it is currently in use, but it was marked for deletion

get.summary:
Retrieve a list of all profiles loaded in your workspace. Use this endpoint to view a preview of each profile so you can quickly assess
available profiles.

post.summary:
Create a new profile based on a selected fingerprint. Supply the required configuration in the request body, then receive the newly
created profile’s full details.

put.summary:
Update an existing profile’s settings by providing the new configuration options in the request body. Use this to modify aspects like
spoofing options or proxy settings, then receive the updated profile details.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

409.description: Cannot edit profile because it is running.

get.summary:
Gets the profile with the specified GUID from the current workspace. On startup, Kameleo will automatically scan and load profiles stored
in your workspace folder on startup.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

delete.summary: Permanently delete a profile from your workspace.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

get.summary: Check the current status of a profile, including its lifecycle and persistence states.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

post.summary:
Starts the selected profile by transitioning its status to 'starting', launching the kernel (Chroma or Junglefox) associated with the
profile, and then changing its status to 'running'.
If there is an error during start-up, it will be 'terminated'. Note that the required kernel might not be available locally, so it may be
downloaded during the profile start.
Optional command line arguments or settings can be passed to the browser engines.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

schema.description: Extra settings and preferences that can be applied to the browser at startup.

post.summary:
Stop a running profile by gracefully shutting down its browser engine and releasing allocated resources. Use this endpoint to transition
the profile from 'running' to 'terminated' state.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

post.summary:
Export a profile to a .kameleo file by providing the target file path in the request body. Use this to back up all profile attributes —
including settings, browsing data, cookies, history, bookmarks, and extensions — for later restoration or migration.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

schema.description: Defines the target location for profile export.

409.description: Cannot export profile if currently running.

post.summary:
Duplicate an existing profile by creating an exact copy with a new unique identifier. Use this endpoint to clone a profile while preserving
all its configuration details and data.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

409.description: Cannot save profile because currently running.

post.summary:
Imports a profile from a .kameleo file, effectively recreating the profile with all its attributes, including settings, browsing data,
cookies, history, bookmarks, and any installed extensions/addons. If the profile is already loaded, the import process will fail.

schema.description: Specifies the source location for profile import.

post.summary:
Upgrades the profile's fingerprint to the most recent browser version available from the server.
This also updates the profile's corresponding kernel if there's a better match available for the new fingerprint.
The new fingerprint is tailored to the profile's current device, browser and operating system. Note that upgrading a profile's kernel will alter its browser fingerprint.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the profile

204.description: No new browser version is available for this profile.

409.description: Cannot upgrade profile because it is running.

AudioSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the audio will be spoofed. Possible values:
'noise': Add some noise to the Audio generation
'block': Completely block the Audio API
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

product.description: Product of the browser. Possible values are 'chrome', 'firefox', 'edge', 'safari'.

domain.description:
The domain attribute signifies the domain for which the cookie is valid and can be submitted with every request for this domain or its
subdomains. If this attribute is not specified, then the hostname of the originating server is used as the default value.

path.description:
The path attribute indicates a URL path that must exist in the requested URL in order to send the Cookie header. The %x2F ("/") character
is considered a directory separator, and subdirectories match as well.

hostOnly.description:
Host Only cookie means that the cookie should be handled by the browser to the server only to the same host/server that firstly sent it to
the browser.

httpOnly.description: When this attribute is set, client-side scripts are not allowed to access the cookie.

secure.description:
A cookie with the Secure attribute is sent to the server only with an encrypted request over the HTTPS protocol, never with unsecured HTTP,
and therefore can't easily be accessed by a man-in-the-middle attacker. Insecure sites (with http: in the URL) can't set cookies with the
Secure attribute.

sameSite.description:
The sameSite attribute lets servers require that a cookie shouldn't be sent with cross-origin requests (where Site is defined by the
registrable domain), which provides some protection against cross-site request forgery attacks (CSRF).
It takes three possible values: Strict, Lax, and None.With Strict, the cookie is sent only to the same site as the one that originated it;
Lax is similar, with an exception for when the user navigates to a URL from an external site, such as by following a link; None has no
restrictions on cross-site requests.

expirationDate.description:
This unix timestamp formatted attribute is used to set persistent cookies. It signifies how long the browser should use the persistent
cookie and when the cookie should be deleted.
If this attribute is not specified, then the lifetime of the cookie is the same as that of browser session, i.e.it will be a non-persistent
cookie.

session.description:
Session cookies are deleted when the current session ends. The browser defines when the "current session" ends, and some browsers use
session restoring when restarting, which can cause session cookies to last indefinitely long.

storeId.description: The ID of the cookie store containing this cookie.

arguments.description:
Command line arguments that can be passed to the browser at startup. This applies to both Chroma and Junglefox browsers. Do not include the
starting double dash (--).

preferences.description:
List of preferences for browsers that can be passed at startup. In case of Chromium based options it will be a user profile preference. In
case of Firefox profiles it will be a preference.

additionalOptions.description: Special options available only in Kameleo, such as device scale factor for mobile emulated profiles. See the docs for more details.

BrowserSettings.description: Extra settings and preferences that can be applied to the browser at startup.

CanvasSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the canvas will be spoofed. Possible values:
'intelligent': Use intelligent canvas spoofing. This will result non-unique canvas fingerprints.
'noise': Add some noise to canvas generation.
'block': Completely block the 2D API.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

domain.description:
The domain attribute signifies the domain for which the cookie is valid and can be submitted with every request for this domain or its
subdomains. If this attribute is not specified, then the hostname of the originating server is used as the default value.

path.description:
The path attribute indicates a URL path that must exist in the requested URL in order to send the Cookie header. The %x2F ("/") character
is considered a directory separator, and subdirectories match as well.

hostOnly.description:
Host Only cookie means that the cookie should be handled by the browser to the server only to the same host/server that firstly sent it to
the browser.

httpOnly.description: When this attribute is set, client-side scripts are not allowed to access the cookie.

A cookie with the Secure attribute is sent to the server only with an encrypted request over the HTTPS protocol, never with unsecured HTTP,
and therefore can't easily be accessed by a man-in-the-middle attacker. Insecure sites (with http: in the URL) can't set cookies with the
Secure attribute.

The sameSite attribute lets servers require that a cookie shouldn't be sent with cross-origin requests (where Site is defined by the

It takes three possible values: Strict, Lax, and None.With Strict, the cookie is sent only to the same site as the one that originated it;

This attribute is used to set persistent cookies. It signifies how long the browser should use the persistent cookie and when the cookie
should be deleted.
If this attribute is not specified, then the lifetime of the cookie is the same as that of browser session, i.e.it will be a non-persistent
cookie.

name.description: Human readable name of the new folder.

fingerprintId.description: The unique identifier of the fingerprint. This references the fingerprint which should be used to build the new profile.

name.description: Sets a human-readable name for the profile, which is modifiable at any time.

folderId.description: Id of the folder the profile should be created in. If null or empty (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) the profile will be created in root.

tags.description: Use tags to categorize profiles by labeling them accordingly.

canvas.description:
Specifies how the canvas will be spoofed. Possible values:
'intelligent': Use intelligent canvas spoofing. This will result non-unique canvas fingerprints.
'noise': Add some noise to canvas generation.
'block': Completely block the 2D API.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

webgl.description:
Specifies how the WebGL will be spoofed. Possible values:
'noise': Add some noise to the WebGL generation
'block': Completely block the 3D API
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

webglMeta.description: Sets how the WebGL Vendor and Renderer will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'.

audio.description:
Specifies how the audio will be spoofed. Possible values:
'noise': Add some noise to the Audio generation
'block': Completely block the Audio API
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

timezone.description: Sets how the Timezone will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'.

geolocation.description: Sets how the Geolocation will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'block', 'off'.

proxy.description:
Sets the Proxy connection settings of the profile. Values can be 'none', 'http', 'socks5', 'ssh'. When it is not set to none, a server must
be provided.

webRtc.description: Sets how the WebRTC will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'block', 'off'.

fonts.description:
Specifies how the fonts will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Spoof fonts based on the browser fingerpint.
'off': Don't spoof fonts, use the real fonts of your machine.

screen.description:
Sets how the Screen will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'. When value is set to manual, a ScreenSize must be
provided.

hardwareConcurrency.description:
Sets how the Hardware Concurrency will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'. When value is set to manual, a numeric value
(1, 2, 4, 8, 12 or 16) must be provided.

deviceMemory.description:
Sets how the Device Memory will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'. When value is set to manual, a numeric value (0.25,
0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8) must be provided.

language.description: Language of the profile as ISO 639-1 language and optionally ISO 3166-1 region code.

startPage.description: This website will be opened in the browser when the profile launches.

passwordManager.description:
Defines whether the browser can save login credentials. Possible values are:
'enabled': Credential saving is allowed.
'disabled': Credential saving is blocked.

extensions.description:
A list of abolute paths from where the profile should load extensions or addons when starting the browser. For chrome and edge use CRX3
format extensions. For firefox use signed xpi format addons.

notes.description: A free text including any notes written by the user.
notes.example: I used this profile for affiliate marketing.

storage.description:
Profile storage property which determines where the profile is stored. The default value is 'local'. When the value is changed the profile
will be migrated.

movedProfiles.description: Ids of the profiles moved to top-level.

type.description: Type of the device. Possible values are: 'desktop', 'mobile'.

name.description: Name of the device. This is only available for mobile profiles.

value.description:
Specifies how the deviceMemory will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically set the values based on the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually set the value in the profile. Valid values: 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

DeviceMemorySpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the deviceMemory will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically set the values based on the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually set the value in the profile. Valid values: 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

path.description: Specifies the file path for exporting the profile.

ExportProfileRequest.description: Defines the target location for profile export.

version.description: The version of the fingerprint. As time passes new fingerprint versions will be introduced. It is recommended to use the latest one.

id.description: The unique identifier of the fingerprint. You can use this as a reference to create a new profile from this fingerprint.

userAgent.description: The user agent of the browser fingerprint.
userAgent.example: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/136.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

device.description: Information about the device of the fingerprint.

os.description: Information about the OS of the fingerprint.

browser.description: Information about the browser of the fingerprint.

webglMeta.description: The GPU details extracted from WebGL parameters.

resolution.description: The screen size of the device in pixels.

fonts.description: A list of font types included in the profile.

Fingerprint.description:
Provides a full view of a fingerprint, which encapsulates real-world browser fingerprint configurations used to
instantiate virtual browser profiles.

id.description: The unique identifier of the fingerprint. You can use this as a reference to create a new profile from this fingerprint.

device.description: Information about the device of the fingerprint.

os.description: Information about the OS of the fingerprint.

browser.description: Information about the browser of the fingerprint.

webglMeta.description: The GPU details of the fingerprint extracted from WebGL parameters.

userAgent.description: The user agent of the fingerprint.

fontsCount.description: The number of fonts in the fingerprint.

FingerprintPreview.description:
Provides a summarized view of a fingerprint, which encapsulates real-world browser fingerprint configurations used to
instantiate virtual browser profiles. This preview aids in selecting the appropriate fingerprint from hundreds of thousands available.

id.description: A unique identifier of the folder.

lastModifiedBy.description: Name of the user who last modified the folder.

createdBy.description: Name of the user who created the folder.

profiles.description: List of profiles in the current folder.

shareAccesses.description: List of users accessing this folder.

FontSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the fonts will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Spoof fonts based on the browser fingerpint.
'off': Don't spoof fonts, use the real fonts of your machine.

value.description:
Specifies how the geolocation will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically set the values based on the IP address
'manual': Manually set the longitude and latitude in the profile
'block': Completely block the Geolocation API
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

extra.description: When the Geolocation spoofing is set to manual these extra settings will be used as well.

GeolocationSpoofingOptions.description: When the Geolocation spoofing is set to manual these extra settings will be used as well.

GeolocationSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the geolocation will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically set the values based on the IP address
'manual': Manually set the longitude and latitude in the profile
'block': Completely block the Geolocation API
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

value.description:
Specifies how the hardwareConcurrency will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically set the values based on the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually set the value in the profile. Valid values: 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 16.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

HardwareConcurrencySpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the hardwareConcurrency will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically set the values based on the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually set the value in the profile. Valid values: 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 16.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

path.description: The file path from which the profile will be imported.

ImportProfileRequest.description: Specifies the source location for profile import.

platform.description: The operating system this kernel supports (e.g: win-x64, osx-arm64).

browser.description: The browser this kernel contains (e.g: chroma, junglefox).

downloadSize.description: Size of the download package in bytes.

diskSize.description: Size of the extracted package on the local file system in bytes or null if the kernel is not installed.

isInstalled.description: Flag indicating if the kernel is locally available (true) or it has to be installed before usage (false).

isInUse.description: Flag indicating if the kernel is currently in use.

isMarkedForRemoval.description: Flag indicating that the kernel will be removed from the local file system when it is not in use.

folders.description: List of top-level folders, each folder may contain nested folders and cloud profiles.

profiles.description: List of profiles not associated with any folder. This includes both cloud and local profiles.

family.description: Specifies the operating system family. Possible values are 'windows', 'macos', 'linux', 'android', 'ios'.

version.description: The specific version of the OS. For example it helps you determine the exact version of the macOS.

platform.description: The OS's platform, indicating the processor architecture (e.g., 64-bit, 32-bit)

PasswordManagerType.description:
Defines whether the browser can save login credentials. Possible values are:
'enabled': Credential saving is allowed.
'disabled': Credential saving is blocked.

value.description: Value of the preference. It can a string or a boolean or an integer

Preference.description: Represents a Key-Value pair where Value can be a string or a boolean or an integer

ProfileLifetimeState.description:
Represents the lifetime states of a profile, indicating which actions
can be performed with the associated browser engine at each state. Possible values are:
- Created: Profile is created; the associated browser engine is not started.
- Starting: The associated browser engine is starting.
- Running: The associated browser engine is currently running.
- Terminating: The associated browser engine is in the process of terminating.
- Terminated: The associated browser engine is not running but has been started at least once.
- Locked: The profile is currently being used by another user.
- Loading: The profile data or the browser engine is syncing with the cloud storage.
- Unknown: State of the profile is undefined.

id.description: A unique identifier of the profile

proxy.description: Proxy connection settings of the profiles. Values can be 'none', 'http', 'socks5', 'ssh'. When it is not set to none, a server is provided.

createdAt.description: Date when the profile was created.

device.description: Device information about the profile. This is derived from the fingerprint.

os.description: Information about the OS of the profile. This is derived from the fingerprint.

browser.description: Information about the browser of the profile. This is derived from the fingerprint.

language.description: Language of the profile as ISO 639-1 language and optionally ISO 3166-1 region code.

storage.description:
Profile storage property which determines where the profile is stored. The default value is 'local'. When the value is changed the profile
will be migrated.

folderId.description:
A unique identifier of the containing folder, or empty (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) if not in a folder.
This will always be empty for locally stored profiles, as only cloud profiles can be added to folders.

ProfilePreview.description: A preview about the profile with some of its properties.

id.description: A unique identifier of the profile

name.description:
Profile name property. The value obtained by file name for existing profiles. For new profiles the value is generated by a random name
generator.

createdAt.description: Date when the profile was created.

fingerprint.description:
Provides a full view of a fingerprint, which encapsulates real-world browser fingerprint configurations used to
instantiate virtual browser profiles.

canvas.description:
Specifies how the canvas will be spoofed. Possible values:
'intelligent': Use intelligent canvas spoofing. This will result non-unique canvas fingerprints.
'noise': Add some noise to canvas generation.
'block': Completely block the 2D API.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

webgl.description:
Specifies how the WebGL will be spoofed. Possible values:
'noise': Add some noise to the WebGL generation
'block': Completely block the 3D API
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

webglMeta.description:
Sets how the WebGL Vendor and Renderer will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': The vendor and renderer values comes from the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually set the vendor and renderer values.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

audio.description:
Specifies how the audio will be spoofed. Possible values:
'noise': Add some noise to the Audio generation
'block': Completely block the Audio API
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

timezone.description: Sets how the Timezone will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'.

geolocation.description: Sets how the Geolocation will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'block', 'off'.

proxy.description: Proxy connection settings of the profiles. Values can be 'none', 'http', 'socks5', 'ssh'. When it is not set to none, a server is provided.

webRtc.description: Sets how the WebRTC will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'block', 'off'.

fonts.description:
Specifies how the fonts will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Spoof fonts based on the browser fingerpint.
'off': Don't spoof fonts, use the real fonts of your machine.

screen.description: Sets how the Screen will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'. When value is set to manual, a ScreenSize must be provided

hardwareConcurrency.description:
Sets how the Hardware Concurrency will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'. When value is set to manual, a
HardwareConcurrencyType must be provided (valid values:1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 16)

deviceMemory.description:
Sets the level of device memory spoofing. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'.
When value is set to manual, a specific amount of device memory must be provided (valid values: 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8)

language.description: Language of the profile as ISO 639-1 language and optionally ISO 3166-1 region code.

startPage.description: This website will be opened in the browser when the profile launches.

passwordManager.description:
Defines whether the browser can save login credentials. Possible values are:
'enabled': Credential saving is allowed.
'disabled': Credential saving is blocked.

extensions.description:
A list of extensions or addons that will be loaded to the profile when the profile is started. For chrome and edge use CRX3 format
extensions. For firefox use signed xpi format addons.

notes.description: A free text including any notes written by the user.
notes.example: I used this profile for ad verification.

storage.description:
Profile storage property which determines where the profile is stored. The default value is 'local'. When the value is changed the profile
will be migrated.

folderId.description: A unique identifier of the containing folder or empty (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) if it is not in folder.

value.description:
Proxy connection settings of the profiles. Possible values:
'none': Direct connection without any proxy.
'http': Use a HTTP proxy for upstream communication.
'socks5': Use a SOCKS5 proxy for upstream communication.
'ssh': Use an SSH connection for upstream communication. Basically a SOCKS5 proxy created at the given SSH host.

extra.description: Represents a server connection. It can be used as a proxy server connection as well.

ProxyConnectionType.description:
Proxy connection settings of the profiles. Possible values:
'none': Direct connection without any proxy.
'http': Use a HTTP proxy for upstream communication.
'socks5': Use a SOCKS5 proxy for upstream communication.
'ssh': Use an SSH connection for upstream communication. Basically a SOCKS5 proxy created at the given SSH host.

currentUsage.description: Indicates the current usage, always a non-negative value.

maximumLimit.description: Indicates the maximum permitted value, with -1 implying no limit.

automated.description: Quota usage of the profiles that are running with an automation framework.

manual.description: Quota usage of the profiles that are running manually (were started from GUI or API).

total.description: Quota usage of the profiles that are running either manually or with an automation framework.

value.description:
Specifies how the screen will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically override the screen resolution based on the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually override the screen resolution.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

extra.description:
The screen size of the device in CSS pixels.
CSS values correspond to bigger physical pixel values when scaled, so provide values accordingly. (Kameleo's default scaling is 1x for desktop and 2x for mobile profiles.)
Examples for 1080p: 1920x1080 (desktop), 412x732 (mobile).

ScreenSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the screen will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically override the screen resolution based on the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually override the screen resolution.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings.

host.description: Gets or sets the hostname where the service is provided from.

port.description: Gets or sets the port where the service is provided from.

id.description:
Gets or sets the identity information provided for the service. This could be a custom id or username or anything which identifies a
resource on the remote service. Use it as a proxy username. This field is optional.

secret.description: Gets or sets the shared secret between the client and the service provider. Use it as a proxy password. This field is optional.

Server.description: Represents a server connection. It can be used as a proxy server connection as well.

role.description: Level of access to the folder.

sharedAt.description: Timestamp when the acess was granted to the user.

shareAccesses.description: List of share accesses to the folder.

users.description: List of users in your team.

roles.description: List of roles you can give the users.

lifetimeState.description:
Represents the lifetime states of a profile, indicating which actions
can be performed with the associated browser engine at each state. Possible values are:
- Created: Profile is created; the associated browser engine is not started.
- Starting: The associated browser engine is starting.
- Running: The associated browser engine is currently running.
- Terminating: The associated browser engine is in the process of terminating.
- Terminated: The associated browser engine is not running but has been started at least once.
- Locked: The profile is currently being used by another user.
- Loading: The profile data or the browser engine is syncing with the cloud storage.
- Unknown: State of the profile is undefined.

value.description:
Specifies how the timezone will be spoofed. Possble values:
'automatic': Timezone is automatically set by the IP
'manual': Timezone is manually overridden in the profile
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

extra.description: When the Timezone spoofing is set to manual the timezone in Iana format is required. For example: America/Grenada

TimezoneSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the timezone will be spoofed. Possble values:
'automatic': Timezone is automatically set by the IP
'manual': Timezone is manually overridden in the profile
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

name.description: Human readable name of the folder.

Profile name property. The value obtained by file name for existing profiles. For new profiles the value is generated by a random name
generator.

folderId.description: Id of the folder the profile should be moved to. If null it won't be changed, if empty (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) it will be moved to root.

canvas.description: Sets how the canvas will be spoofed. Values can be 'intelligent', 'noise', 'block', 'off'.

webgl.description: Sets how the WebGL will be spoofed. Values can be 'noise', 'block', 'off'.

webglMeta.description:
Sets how the WebGL Vendor and Renderer will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': The vendor and renderer values comes from the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually set the vendor and renderer values.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

audio.description: Sets how the Audio will be spoofed. Values can be 'noise', 'block', 'off'.

timezone.description: Sets how the Timezone will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'.

geolocation.description: Sets how the Geolocation will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'block', 'off'.

proxy.description:
Proxy connection settings of the profiles. Values can be 'none', 'http', 'socks5', 'ssh'. When it is not set to none, a server must be
provided.

webRtc.description: Sets how the WebRTC will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'block', 'off'.

fonts.description: Sets how the Fonts will be spoofed. Values can be 'enabled', 'disabled'.

screen.description: Sets how the Screen will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'. When value is set to manual, a ScreenSize must be provided

hardwareConcurrency.description:
Sets how the Hardware Concurrency will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'. When value is set to manual, a
HardwareConcurrencyType must be provided (valid values:1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 16)

deviceMemory.description:
Sets how the Device Memory will be spoofed. Values can be 'automatic', 'manual', 'off'. When value is set to manual, a DeviceMemoryType
must be provided (valid values: 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8)

language.description: Language of the profile as ISO 639-1 language and optionally ISO 3166-1 region code.

startPage.description: This website will be opened in the browser when the profile launches.

passwordManager.description: Indicates whether the browser supports saving login credentials.

extensions.description:
A list of extensions or addons should be loaded to the browser when starting the profile. For extensions that are added now, it should be
an absolute path.
For extensions already added to the profile in a previous update, the name is only enough.
For chrome and edge use CRX3 format extensions. For firefox use signed xpi format addons.

notes.description: A free text including any notes written by the user.
notes.example: I used this profile for affiliate marketing.

storage.description:
Profile storage property which determines where the profile is stored. The default value is 'local'. When the value is changed the profile
will be migrated.

userId.description: Unique identifier for the user in Guid v4 format.

displayName.description: The display name of the user, updated on each login.

email.description: The email address of the user.

emailConfirmed.description: Indicates if the user's email address has been verified.

subscriptionEnd.description: The subscription end date for the user. If the user is part of a team, this is the team's subscription end date.

plan.description: The subscription plan associated with the user.

capabilities.description: List of features available to the user based on their current subscription or their team's subscription.

gracePeriod.description: Indicates if the user's subscription is currently in a grace period and requires instant renewal.

lastAppLogin.description: The date and time of the user's last login via the app.

workspaceFolder.description: Path to the user's workspace folder where profiles are stored. Modifying these files outside of Kameleo is strictly prohibited.

localStorage.description: Current usage and maximum limit for local profiles.

cloudStorage.description: Current usage and maximum limit for cloud profiles.

hasTeamSubscription.description: Indicates if the user has a team subscription. This can also be true if the user does not have any team members yet.

teamId.description: The team ID if the user is part of a team.

teamRole.description: The user's role within the team, such as 'owner' or 'member'.

runningUserProfiles.description: Current usage and maximum limit of running manual and automated profiles for the logged-in user.

runningTeamProfiles.description: Current usage and maximum limit of running manual and automated profiles across the user's team.

runningTenantProfiles.description: Current usage and maximum limit of running manual and automated profiles across the tenant.

profileMinutes.description: Current usage and maximum limit of profile minutes across the tenant.

value.description:
Specifies how the WebRTC will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically set the webRTC public IP by the IP
'manual': Manually override the webRTC public IP and private IP in the profile
'block': Block the WebRTC functionality
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

extra.description: When the WebRTC spoofing is set to manual these extra settings will be used as well.

publicIp.description: The WebRTC public IP address of the machine.

WebRtcSpoofingOptions.description: When the WebRTC spoofing is set to manual these extra settings will be used as well.

WebRtcSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the WebRTC will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': Automatically set the webRTC public IP by the IP
'manual': Manually override the webRTC public IP and private IP in the profile
'block': Block the WebRTC functionality
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

vendor.description: The UnmaskedVendor field from WebGL context

renderer.description: The UnmaskedRenderer field from WebGL context
renderer.example: ANGLE (AMD, AMD Radeon(TM) RX Vega 11 Graphics Direct3D11 vs_5_0 ps_5_0, D3D11)

value.description:
Specifies how the WebGL vendor and renderer will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': The vendor and renderer values comes from the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually configure WebGL metadata. For optimal results, choose a video card model similar to your device's to ensure realistic
profile masking.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

extra.description: When the WebGL Meta spoofing is used, these settings can override the values in the fingerprint.

renderer.example: ANGLE (Intel(R) HD Graphics 630 Direct3D11 vs_5_0 ps_5_0)

WebglMetaSpoofingOptions.description: When the WebGL Meta spoofing is used, these settings can override the values in the fingerprint.

WebglMetaSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the WebGL vendor and renderer will be spoofed. Possible values:
'automatic': The vendor and renderer values comes from the fingerprint.
'manual': Manually configure WebGL metadata. For optimal results, choose a video card model similar to your device's to ensure realistic
profile masking.
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings

WebglSpoofingType.description:
Specifies how the WebGL will be spoofed. Possible values:
'noise': Add some noise to the WebGL generation
'block': Completely block the 3D API
'off': Turn off the spoofing, use the original settings
